heading: Health care positions
label: 5371 Health care positions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 1162(b), “Sec. 2(b)”, .Oct. 4, 1961Pub. L. 87–367, § 20275 Stat. 790, , .Oct. 11, 1962Pub. L. 87–793, § 1001(a)(1)76 Stat. 863The authority to fix rates of pay is added on authority of former section 1161, which is carried into section 3104.For repeal of the , , as amended, see revision note for section 3104.Act of Aug. 1, 1947, ch. 43361 Stat. 715Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

Amendments1992—Subsec. (b). substituted “chapter 74” for “chapter 73” in introductory provisions and inserted “subchapter V of chapter 55,” after “61,” in pars. (1) and (2).Pub. L. 102–3781990— amended section generally, substituting designated provisions directing that Office of Personnel Management may provide that chapter 73 of title 38 provisions apply to certain health care professionals for undesignated text authorizing agency heads to fix pay rates for scientific and professional positions at between GS–16 and GS–18 rates.Pub. L. 101–5091978—, substituted “Office of Personnel Management” for “Civil Service Commission”.Pub. L. 95–454, § 906(a)(2)

Effective Date of 1990 AmendmentAmendment by effective on such date as the President shall determine, but not earlier than 90 days, and not later than 180 days, after , see section 529 [title III, § 305] of , set out as a note under .Pub. children.label: a
children.text: For the purposes of this section, “health care” means direct patient-care services or services incident to direct patient-care services.
in lieu of any provision of chapter 51 or 61, subchapter V of chapter 55, or any other provision of this chapter; or
notwithstanding any lack of specific authority for a matter with respect to which chapter 51 or 61, subchapter V of chapter 55, or this chapter, relates.
to which chapter 51 applies, excluding any Senior Executive Service position and any position in the Federal Bureau of Investigation and Drug Enforcement Administration Senior Executive Service; and
